message:
chore(beads): sync issues.jsonl

Pick up concurrent beads changes from the swarm:

- bd-881az (new, open): REVIEW [MEDIUM] — readdir holds DIR_REGISTRY
  mutex across sys_getdents64, causing cross-directory contention when
  any one directory's readdir stalls on NFS/FUSE. Classic Class 1
  "blocking syscall while holding a global lock" anti-pattern. Fix
  direction noted in the bead (per-DirState mutexes, or swap-remove +
  sys_getdents64 outside the lock + re-insert).

- bd-ijybx (new, open): REVIEW [HIGH] — pidfd_spawn/pidfd_spawnp use
  posix_spawn followed by pidfd_open(child_pid, 0), so a short-lived
  child whose PID is recycled between those two calls returns a pidfd
  pointing at the wrong process. Defeats the whole point of pidfd's
  race-free process reference. Fix direction: reimplement via clone3
  with CLONE_PIDFD (already supported at frankenlibc-core syscall/mod.rs:375),
  or SIGCHLD-block the window and verify start time.

- bd-ju24y: close metadata cleanup (closed_at moved to top level,
  trailing UTC marker normalized) — no semantic change, just noise
  from the JSON round-trip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-881az","title":"REVIEW: [MEDIUM] readdir holds DIR_REGISTRY mutex across sys_getdents64 — cross-dir contention on slow FS","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/dirent_abi.rs:153 readdir acquires DIR_REGISTRY.lock() and holds it through state.get_mut() + sys_getdents64 at line 192. sys_getdents64 is a blocking syscall (can take seconds on NFS/FUSE). While it blocks, every other thread's opendir/readdir/closedir on ANY directory blocks on the mutex. Classic Class 1 anti-pattern: blocking call while holding lock. Fix (larger scope): replace the global Mutex<Option<HashMap>> with per-DirState mutexes or a sharded map; OR swap-remove state under the lock, call sys_getdents64 without the lock, then re-insert. Not a correctness bug (single-dir readdir works fine) but a scalability defect that manifests as unexplained multi-second stalls when one dir operation is slow.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:51:10.779691963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:51:10.779691963Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Fix shipped in 71ffa897. Filing retroactively per Tick-95 request to track each harness defect explicitly. Not a library bug — the frankenlibc ABI was correct throughout; only the fuzz harness invariants needed tightening.","notes":"Fixed in 71ffa897 — store cond's clock in CondSlot and use it in clock_gettime for timedwait.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:30:13.052078812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -725,6 +726,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ie8zc","title":"atoi clamps to INT_MAX on overflow instead of truncating per POSIX (int)strtol equivalence","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/conversion.rs\` atoi() clamps
the strtol result into i32 range:

    if val > i32::MAX as i64 { i32::MAX }
    else if val < i32::MIN as i64 { i32::MIN }
    else { val as i32 }

POSIX SUSv4 specifies that atoi(str) is equivalent to
\`(int) strtol(str, (char **)NULL, 10)\`. \`(int) long_val\` on
2's-complement machines (all targets we support) is a TRUNCATION
cast, not a saturating clamp.

Observed divergence (found by fuzz_stdlib):
- Input: \"99999999999999\" (fourteen 9s)
- strtol → 99999999999999 (fits in i64, no overflow, errno unchanged)
- glibc atoi: (int) 99999999999999 = 0x107A3FFF = 276447231
- Our atoi: clamps, returns INT_MAX = 2147483647

## Fix

Replace the if-else clamp with a bare \`val as i32\` cast. C
standard says overflow behavior is UB; POSIX equivalence with
(int)strtol pins it down to truncation on 2's-complement.
matches glibc's observable behavior.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P2). Any C program that relies on atoi's documented
POSIX equivalence to \`(int)strtol\` gets a different value from
us than from glibc when the source string overflows i32. Likely
rare in production (most callers already bound their input), but
a documented divergence from glibc.","notes":"

Fixed in 6d29fb13: atoi returns `val as i32` (truncation cast) instead of clamping to INT_MAX, matching POSIX SUSv4 equivalence with (int)strtol. Verified: 60s fuzz_stdlib clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:01:52.755209221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:14:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:14:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-ijybx","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] pidfd_spawn uses posix_spawn + pidfd_open — PID-reuse race defeats pidfd guarantees","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs:5573 pidfd_spawn implements via posix_spawn + pidfd_open(child_pid, 0). Between those two calls the child may exit and the PID be recycled, so pidfd_open resolves to the wrong process. This defeats the entire point of pidfd (the race-free reference-a-process primitive). glibc 2.39's pidfd_spawn uses clone3 with CLONE_PIDFD atomically; our CLONE_PIDFD support already exists (frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs:375). Fix direction: re-implement pidfd_spawn/pidfd_spawnp via clone3(CLONE_PIDFD) + exec, OR at minimum block SIGCHLD across the window and check the child's start time on pidfd_open. Reach: any LD_PRELOAD'd app that calls pidfd_spawn on short-lived children (common in container orchestration) may signal the wrong process. Same issue in pidfd_spawnp line 5603.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:50:02.842113919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:50:02.842113919Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-imo7","title":"Metamorphic tests for raw syscall signal semantics","description":"The syscall veneer tests exercise signalfd4 and timer sigevent but lack direct MR coverage for sys_kill/sys_tgkill/sys_tkill/sys_rt_sigaction/sys_rt_sigprocmask roundtrip semantics. Add: (1) kill(self, 0) succeeds (POSIX null signal permission check), (2) kill(self, invalid_sig) fails with EINVAL, (3) tgkill(pid, tid, 0) ≡ tkill(tid, 0) parity for self-thread, (4) rt_sigprocmask get-set-restore is invertive. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/syscall_veneer_test.rs. One MR per commit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:53:42.606540761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:56:47.761683183Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:56:47.759781481Z","close_reason":"Added 4 metamorphic raw-syscall signal tests across 3 commits: 58cfdf4 (kill self null-signal + out-of-range rejection), f7f1055 (tgkill/tkill parity + tgid mismatch ESRCH), 01975f0 (rt_sigprocmask invertive get/set/restore). Fills veneer coverage gap — previously only signalfd4/timer_create_sigevent tested signal-related syscalls.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-jqy64","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create and join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS plus propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create, join, detach, self, and equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:20.777550950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:48:11.443759199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"]}
 {"id":"bd-jtix","title":"string: execute wide_string_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/wide_string_ops_conformance_test.rs still exercises fixtures only through the in-process executor. Add isolated harness subprocess coverage for tests/conformance/fixtures/wide_string_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity per mode, and fix any divergence exposed by the matrix path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.305846872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:43:59.323959467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:43:59.321768510Z","close_reason":"Added wide_string_ops_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix in commit be2558b — dispatches all 16 fixture cases through harness conformance-matrix-case subprocess (mirrors search_ops pattern). All cases pass via both in-process and subprocess paths. cargo check/clippy/test/fmt clean via rch + isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR; ubs exit 0.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-ju24y","title":"REVIEW: [MEDIUM] printf %g does not switch to %e when rounding overflows precision","description":"crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs::format_g for value=999999.5 with default precision 6 renders '1000000' (7 digits), while glibc renders '1e+06'. The C11 7.21.6.1 para 8 rule: %g uses %e if exp<-4 OR exp>=precision. The pre-round exp check picks %f, but after rounding-half-to-even the integer part grows from 6 to 7 digits (999999.5 -> 1000000), which violates the precision contract; glibc re-classifies to %e in this case. Fix: after %f-style rendering, count the integer-part digits; if they exceed precision, re-render via format_e. Also confirmed divergence exists on same half-to-even rounding boundary (e.g. 99.95 with precision 3: glibc -> '100', ours would be '100' too — OK — but 999999.5 with default precision is the clean case).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:40.367348672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:55:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:55:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 7df551a5: format_g detects post-round integer overflow and switches to %e. Regression test: format_g(999999.5, 6, false, false) == '1e+06'. 37/37 printf tests pass."}
+{"id":"bd-ju24y","title":"REVIEW: [MEDIUM] printf %g does not switch to %e when rounding overflows precision","description":"crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs::format_g for value=999999.5 with default precision 6 renders '1000000' (7 digits), while glibc renders '1e+06'. The C11 7.21.6.1 para 8 rule: %g uses %e if exp<-4 OR exp>=precision. The pre-round exp check picks %f, but after rounding-half-to-even the integer part grows from 6 to 7 digits (999999.5 -> 1000000), which violates the precision contract; glibc re-classifies to %e in this case. Fix: after %f-style rendering, count the integer-part digits; if they exceed precision, re-render via format_e. Also confirmed divergence exists on same half-to-even rounding boundary (e.g. 99.95 with precision 3: glibc -> '100', ours would be '100' too — OK — but 999999.5 with default precision is the clean case).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:40.367348672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:55:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:55:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 7df551a5: format_g detects post-round integer overflow and switches to %e. Regression test: format_g(999999.5, 6, false, false) == '1e+06'. 37/37 printf tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ju7","title":"Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)","description":"Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1101,"issue_id":"bd-ju7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}
